note: Per-DIRECTION ratchet floors for PRODUCTION (non-test/fixture) sharded≡exact RESOLVED-edge divergences on the unified resolution model (ADR-0033, supersedes ADR-0032). functionsOnly{Exact,Sharded} MUST be 0 (hard failure). Each direction is gated independently — EXCEEDING any floor fails graph-equivalence-check (a NEW divergence on the unified model); a decrease passes with a tighten hint. Gating per-direction (not a flat total) so a fixed conflict cannot mask a new phantom. After Phase 3 (one model, Option A): the single-program (exact) build recovers cross-package edges via the SAME boundary linker (resolveCrossBoundaryCalls) the sharded build runs post-merge (exact = the 1-shard case). DIRECTIONS & RESIDUAL CLASSES (12 total, all tracked): (1) phantom=1 (sharded resolved, exact declined) — packages/graph/engine/src/cli/dashboard-data.ts:44 `scope.graph?.rules.getAll()`: sharded CORRECTLY resolves to RuleRegistry.getAll; exact under-resolves through the optional-chain (`?.`) property access. NOT a fabrication — a real edge exact misses; direction confirms 'exact bound insufficient here'. Follow-up: exact optional-chain property-access resolution. (2) decline=0 (exact resolved, sharded declined) — none. (3) conflict=11 (both resolved, DIFFERENT targets) — cross-package SAME-NAME registry methods (find/getAll/register across CheckRegistry/RuleRegistry/AdapterRegistry/RecipeRegistry) where the two engines pick different same-name occurrences; a genuine disambiguation bug (at least one engine is wrong), the load-bearing residual, tracked for follow-up. KNOWN OUT-OF-SCOPE (both engines decline → invisible to this differential gate, guarded by the pinned-corpus resolution-completeness floor in resolution-completeness-floor.test.ts): arrow-property method calls like logger.info() where findCatalogEntry cannot reproduce the arrow occurrence's hash. DEFERRED (explicit): clean-checkout parity for WORKSPACE imports — exact still resolves @scope/pkg through the dep's built dist/*.d.ts to reach the boundary linker, so `rm -rf packages/*/dist` drops those edges in exact (sharded's syntactic extractor is dist-independent; the file+name pin made RELATIVE imports clean-safe in both). Source-as-surface deferred to follow-up; Phase 6's clean-checkout assertion is scoped to the relative class.
